content.problemStatement:
Chef had three sequences of real numbers k1,k2,‚Ä¶,kNk_1, k_2, \ldots, k_Nk1‚Äã,k2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,kN‚Äã, c1,c2,‚Ä¶,cNc_1, c_2, \ldots, c_Nc1‚Äã,c2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,cN‚Äã and x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äã. Unfortunately, he lost the sequence xxx; the only thing he remembers about it is that
x1‚ãÖk1+x2‚ãÖk2+‚Ä¶+xN‚ãÖkN=0‚Äâ.x_1 \cdot k_1 + x_2 \cdot k_2 + \ldots + x_N \cdot k_N = 0 \,.x1‚Äã‚ãÖk1‚Äã+x2‚Äã‚ãÖk2‚Äã+‚Ä¶+xN‚Äã‚ãÖkN‚Äã=0.

Chef's favourite expression is
x1+c1+x2+c2+‚Ä¶+xN+cN‚Äâ.\sqrt{x_1+c_1} + \sqrt{x_2+c_2} + \ldots + \sqrt{x_N+c_N} \,.x1‚Äã+c1‚Äã‚Äã+x2‚Äã+c2‚Äã‚Äã+‚Ä¶+xN‚Äã+cN‚Äã‚Äã.
Its value is defined only if xi+ci‚â•0x_i+c_i \ge 0xi‚Äã+ci‚Äã‚â•0 for each valid iii. Let's denote the maximum value of this expression  (over all sequences xxx such that it is defined) by FFF.

Help Chef find FFF and a sequence x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äã such that the value of Chef's expression is FFF, or determine that there is no solution (the value of Chef's expression is always undefined).
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
**Subtask #2 (15 points):** ki=1k_i = 1ki‚Äã=1 and ci=0c_i = 0ci‚Äã=0 for each valid iii
**Subtask #3 (15 points):** ki=1k_i = 1ki‚Äã=1 for each valid iii
**Subtask #4 (15 points):** ci=0c_i = 0ci‚Äã=0 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN real numbers k1,k2,‚Ä¶,kNk_1, k_2, \ldots, k_Nk1‚Äã,k2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,kN‚Äã.
The third line contains NNN real numbers c1,c2,‚Ä¶,cNc_1, c_2, \ldots, c_Nc1‚Äã,c2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,cN‚Äã.
All real numbers are given with exactly two digits after the decimal point.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, if there is no solution, print a single line containing one integer ‚àí1-1‚àí1. Otherwise, print a single line containing N+1N+1N+1 space-separated real numbers F,x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNF, x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_NF,x1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äã.

It can be proved that if a solution exists, then it is unique. Your answer will be considered correct if the absolute or relative error of each number on the output does not exceed 10‚àí210^{‚àí2}10‚àí2.

Explanation:
Example case 1: The optimal solution is x=(28.6,2.4,‚àí49/15,‚àí5.9)x = (28.6, 2.4, -49/15, -5.9)x=(28.6,2.4,‚àí49/15,‚àí5.9). Then, x1‚ãÖk1+x2‚ãÖk2+x3‚ãÖk3+x4‚ãÖk4=28.6‚ãÖ1+2.4‚ãÖ2‚àí49‚ãÖ3/15+(‚àí5.9)‚ãÖ4=0x_1 \cdot k_1 + x_2 \cdot k_2 + x_3 \cdot k_3 + x_4 \cdot k_4 = 28.6 \cdot 1 + 2.4 \cdot 2 - 49 \cdot 3 / 15 + (-5.9) \cdot 4 = 0x1‚Äã‚ãÖk1‚Äã+x2‚Äã‚ãÖk2‚Äã+x3‚Äã‚ãÖk3‚Äã+x4‚Äã‚ãÖk4‚Äã=28.6‚ãÖ1+2.4‚ãÖ2‚àí49‚ãÖ3/15+(‚àí5.9)‚ãÖ4=0, and F=28.6+5+2.4+6+‚àí49/15+7+‚àí5.9+8‚âê12.076F = \sqrt{28.6+5}+\sqrt{2.4+6}+\sqrt{-49/15+7}+\sqrt{-5.9+8} \doteq 12.076F=28.6+5‚Äã+2.4+6‚Äã+‚àí49/15+7‚Äã+‚àí5.9+8‚Äã‚âê12.076. This is the maximum value of Chef's expression.
Example case 2: For each iii, cic_ici‚Äã is negative, but xi+cix_i+c_ixi‚Äã+ci‚Äã must be non-negative, so xix_ixi‚Äã must be positive. However, all kik_iki‚Äã are also positive, so x1k1+x2k2+‚Ä¶+xNkNx_1 k_1 + x_2 k_2 + \ldots + x_N k_Nx1‚Äãk1‚Äã+x2‚Äãk2‚Äã+‚Ä¶+xN‚ÄãkN‚Äã cannot be 000. There is no solution.
